a10.d: SHAP, LIME, counterfactuals with dedicated abstraction.
a11.d: Train, predict, explain, explore, convert, generate, evaluate, optimize.
a2.d: Random Forest to transformers. Subclass + schema, ready for HPO.
a3.d: LLMs and image as first-class citizens.
a4.d: Classification, regression, translation. Auto-appears in UI.
a5.d: Optuna + HyperOpt. Bayesian, evolutionary, QMC strategies.
a6.d: CSV, JSON, images, audio. Each loader declares its format.
a8.d: Typed transformations between formats and representations.
abs.lead: Each functional role has its base class. Twenty to forty lines for a new component. The UI emerges from the schema.
cat.lead: Each model is a subclass of BaseModel with its Pydantic schema. The UI generates itself. Filter by task type below.
r.abr: Focuses on samples with high prediction errors.
r.alb: Pre-trained ALBERT for efficient English text classification.
r.bert: Pre-trained BERT for English text classification.
r.bertin: BERTIN (Spanish RoBERTa) for Spanish text classification.
r.beto: Pre-trained BETO for Spanish text classification.
r.bow: Bag-of-words vectorizer with a DashAI tabular classifier.
r.cn.canny: ControlNet with Canny edge conditioning on SDXL.
r.cn.depth: ControlNet with depth conditioning on SD1.5.
r.cn.hed: ControlNet with HED edge conditioning on SD1.5.
r.cn.pose: ControlNet with OpenPose conditioning on SD1.5.
r.de: Encoder with disentangled attention. State of the art on GLUE.
r.dtc: Axis-aligned decision rules learned from data.
r.dum: Baseline: makes predictions ignoring input features.
r.elec: Pre-trained ELECTRA for efficient English text classification.
r.etr: Fully randomised decision tree splits for regression.
r.gnb: Naive Bayes classifier based on Bayes' theorem.
r.hgbc: Histogram-based gradient boosting for large datasets.
r.lsvc: Linear SVC with Platt-scaling for probabilities.
r.lsvr: Linear kernel SVM for regression on large datasets.
r.m2m: Multilingual seq2seq for configurable language pairs.
r.mb: Modern encoder with 8k token context length.
r.mbert: Multilingual BERT for cross-lingual text classification.
r.mini: Lightweight MiniLM for English text classification.
r.mlp: Multilayer neural network. Entry point to tabular deep learning.
r.mlpr: Single hidden-layer MLP regressor in PyTorch.
r.mx: Mixture of Experts. GPT-3.5 quality at lower cost.
r.nl: No Language Left Behind, 200+ languages. Seq2seq.
r.qw: Alibaba LLM with solid multilingual support.
r.rf: Random forest over scikit-learn. Native HPO.
r.rfr: Averages predictions from multiple decision trees.
r.rg: L2 linear regression. Stable under high collinearity.
r.rob: Pre-trained RoBERTa for English text classification.
r.sd3: V3 with MMDiT, better prompt adherence.
r.sdxlt: Distilled SDXL for real time generation.
r.svc: SVM that maximises the margin between classes.
r.xlnet: Pre-trained XLNet for English text classification.
